detailed thinking on

You are a meticulous legal analyst supporting Fortress Prime's defense team. Answer ONLY from the CASE EVIDENCE blocks provided. Cite the bracketed source filename in each chunk header for every factual claim. If the evidence does not answer a question, say so plainly — never invent.
user:
TASK: Identify and analyze each cause of action that the plaintiff has asserted against the defendant. For each claim: (a) the legal elements the plaintiff must prove, (b) the evidentiary support drawn from the chunks below, and (c) any apparent gaps in plaintiff's proof.

CASE EVIDENCE:

[#13 Joint Preliminary Statement.pdf]
This is a federal civil action filed in the Northern District of Georgia (Case No. 2:21-CV-00226-RWS, Hon. Richard W. Story). The plaintiff (a Georgia LLC) alleges three causes of action against the individual defendant: (1) breach of contract under the 92 Fish Trap Road purchase-and-sale agreement (the 'Agreement'), (2) breach of warranty of title regarding an undisclosed encroachment, and (3) negligent misrepresentation of material facts at and prior to closing. The Agreement specified a closing date of on-or-before June 1, 2021. The amendment required that the Access Easements must be written by a Licensed Georgia attorney prior to, or at, closing. Precedent was never satisfied; consequently the 92 Fish Trap Road Agreement lapsed without closing.

[#65-1 7IL's BIS MSJ.pdf]
Plaintiff filed a brief in support of summary judgment on its breach-of-contract count, asserting that the defendant's failure to convey title constituted total breach. Plaintiff seeks specific performance, money damages, and attorney's fees under O.C.G.A. § 13-6-11. As of July 11, 2023, defendant has earned income of $134,575.86 from the Fish Trap Property and $170,706.01 from the River Heights Property. Plaintiff argues defendant is liable for these amounts as restitution.

[5464474_Exhibit_3_GaryKnight(23945080.1).pdf]
Defendant's Exhibit 3 (¶¶ 73-76): An encroachment was discovered prior to the closing date. Plaintiff refused to accept 92 Fish Trap Road with the encroachment, and the closing attorney refused to close the transaction. Consequently, the 92 Fish Trap Road Contract expired without meeting the closing deadline. The encroachment was not disclosed by defendant prior to plaintiff's tender; defendant maintains the encroachment was not known to him.

[#64-6 Knight's Resp. to Roggs.pdf]
Defense counsel response: Terry Wilson (closing attorney) stated to Scott Kiker that the 92 Fish Trap Road property could not close due to the encroachment. Defendant's counter-claim asserts that plaintiff's refusal to accept title constituted plaintiff's own breach.

FORMAT: Markdown table with columns (a) claim element, (b) evidence chunk reference, (c) gap analysis. One table per cause of action. Do NOT use first-person prose. Do NOT include <think> blocks in the output.
